ham.subject: Updated Company Leave Policy Effective July 1, 2026
ham.body:
Dear Team,

I hope you are all doing well. As part of our ongoing efforts to keep our workplace policies clear and up-to-date, we are pleased to announce several important updates to the Company Leave Policy that will take effect on July 1, 2026.

Key Highlights:
- **Paid Time Off (PTO)**: The annual PTO entitlement has been increased by 5 days for all full‑time employees, reflecting our commitment to work‑life balance.
- **Sick Leave Accrual**: Sick days will now accrue on a monthly basis rather than annually, allowing for more flexible use.
- **Remote Work Flexibility**: Employees may now request up to 10 remote workdays per year without needing departmental approval, subject to project commitments.
- **Family & Parental Leave**: Maternity, paternity, and adoption leave durations have been extended to 12 weeks, with additional unpaid leave options available.
- **Vacation Scheduling**: All vacation requests should now be submitted through the HR portal by at least 14 days in advance to ensure adequate staffing.

Please review the full policy document for detailed information and FAQs. You can access the updated policy at <URL>.

We encourage you to read through the changes carefully and reach out to HR with any questions or concerns. Your feedback helps us create a supportive and transparent workplace.

Thank you for your continued dedication and cooperation.

Best regards,

Sarah L. Mitchell
Director of Human Resources

---

If you would like a hard copy of the policy or need assistance accessing the HR portal, please contact HR Support at <URL> or call 555‑123‑4567.

spam.subject: URGENT: Immediate Action Required - IT Ticket #123456
spam.body:
To: All Employees
From: IT Help Desk <helpdesk@company.com>
Subject: URGENT: Immediate Action Required - IT Ticket #123456

Dear [Employee Name],

Our automated monitoring system has flagged a potential security breach associated with your workstation (Ticket #123456). The following activities have been detected:

- Multiple failed login attempts
- Unauthorized access to confidential files
- Anomalous outbound network traffic

To protect your data and the integrity of our network, you must complete the following steps within the next 15 minutes:

1. **Reset your password** – Click the link below to create a new, strong password.
   <URL>

2. **Verify your account details** – Confirm your user ID, department, and contact information.
   <URL>

3. **Run a security scan** – Download the provided scanner tool and execute a full system check.
   <URL>

Failure to comply within the specified timeframe will result in temporary suspension of your workstation and may trigger a full security audit. If you believe this is a mistake or you have already taken the required actions, please reply to this email with the subject line "Ticket #123456 - Resolved".

Thank you for your prompt attention to this critical matter.

Sincerely,

IT Help Desk
Security Operations Team
